name: Root-cause analysis

problemSignals.description: The same failure or symptom recurs after local remediation.
Multiple plausible contributing factors lack discriminating evidence.

requiredEvidence.description: Time-ordered observations, changes, controls, and recovery actions.
Reproduction, counterfactual, comparison, or intervention evidence for candidate causes.

assumptions: The analyzed effect is operationally defined
Contributing conditions are not mislabeled as a single universal root

deterministicSteps.action: Define the effect, scope, occurrence window, and non-occurrence comparison.
Enumerate candidate causal chains and distinguish observations, assumptions, and missing evidence.
Apply the declared causal tests and retain supported, rejected, and unresolved hypotheses separately.

outputs.description: Traceable candidate causal chains and evidence gaps.
Supported, rejected, and unresolved causes with test evidence.

whenAll: Intervention or reproduction supports the chain
Multiple independent observations support the chain

contraindications: The request is to enumerate prospective failure modes rather than explain an observed effect
Evidence is limited to an unaudited opinion or post hoc story
